name: Varanasi (Kashi / Banaras)
notes: One of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in the world; Shiva's city.

name: Ayodhya
notes: Birthplace of Rama; already has a general ayodhya.mdx site page, this is its dedicated CITY page.

name: Vrindavan
notes: Krishna's childhood playground; part of the wider Braj region alongside Mathura and Barsana.

name: Mathura
notes: Krishna's birthplace; distinct from but adjoining Vrindavan within Braj.

name: Haridwar
notes: Gateway to the Char Dham; one of the four Kumbh Mela sites.

name: Ujjain
notes: Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga city; one of the four Kumbh Mela sites.

name: Dwarka
notes: Krishna's kingdom capital; one of the Char Dham of India.

name: Puri
notes: Jagannath Temple city; one of the Char Dham of India.

name: Rishikesh
notes: Yoga capital and gateway to the Char Dham of Uttarakhand.

name: Pushkar
notes: Site of the only major Brahma temple in India, beside its sacred lake.

name: Prayagraj (Allahabad)
notes: Triveni Sangam city; one of the four Kumbh Mela sites.

name: Nashik
notes: Trimbakeshwar Jyotirlinga city; one of the four Kumbh Mela sites.

name: Gaya
notes: Vishnupad Temple city, centre of pind daan ancestral rites; adjoins (but is distinct from) Bodh Gaya.
